Learn AI + LLMs

Real-World Use Cases
Home 01 LLMs 02 Prompts 03 API 04 Tools 05 Embeddings 06 RAG 07 Agents 08 Structured 09 Streaming 10 Evals 11 Ethics 12 Capstone Use Cases

15 AI Use Cases for Journalism

Practical AI applications for newsrooms, investigations, and content workflows. Each card shows a real implementation pattern with architecture and tooling.

01 / NLP

Document Summarizer

AP, Reuters, Bloomberg
1
Input: 50-page PDF report
↓
2
Chunk → Summarize each section
↓
3
Output: 3-paragraph executive summary
  • Map-reduce pattern: split long docs, summarize chunks, merge
  • Chain-of-density prompting for progressively refined summaries
  • Preserves key quotes, dates, and figures from source material
map-reduce prompt engineering PDF parsing
02 / VERIFICATION

Source Verification Tool

Bellingcat, NYT Visual Investigations
Reporter
Verify: "City budget increased 40% in 2024"
AI Verifier
Searching city budget records... Found FY2024 report: budget was $4.2B (FY2023: $3.1B). Actual increase: 35.5%. Claim is approximately correct but overstates by ~4.5 points.
  • RAG pipeline with government document database
  • Cross-references claims against primary sources
  • Returns confidence score and source citations
RAG tool use fact-checking
03 / LEGAL

FOIA Request Generator

MuckRock, IRE, ProPublica
1
Reporter describes what records they need
↓
2
AI generates legally compliant FOIA letter
↓
3
Includes correct agency, statutes, fee waiver
  • Templates for federal, state, and local FOIA requests
  • Auto-identifies correct agency and applicable statutes
  • Generates fee waiver justification for journalists
structured output legal templates prompt chaining
04 / NLP

Interview Transcript Analyzer

The Intercept, NPR
Key Claims
12
Verifiable statements extracted
Topics
5
Budget, housing, transit, ...
Quotes
8
Publication-ready quotes
Follow-ups
3
Suggested follow-up questions
  • Extracts claims, quotes, topics, and suggested follow-ups
  • Speaker diarization to attribute quotes correctly
  • Highlights contradictions with previous statements
extraction structured output NLP
05 / VERIFICATION

Fact-check Pipeline

PolitiFact, Snopes, FullFact
1
Extract verifiable claims from text
↓
2
Search knowledge base + web for evidence
↓
3
Rate: True / Mostly True / False + evidence
  • Three-step: claim extraction → evidence retrieval → verdict
  • Agent loop with web search and document retrieval tools
  • Human-in-the-loop for final publication decision
agents tool use RAG
06 / ANALYTICS

Sentiment Analysis Dashboard

Social media monitors, newsrooms
Positive
42%
Neutral
31%
Negative
27%
1,247 posts analyzed · Last 24 hours
  • Batch classification of social media posts and comments
  • Tracks sentiment trends over time for breaking stories
  • Detects sarcasm and nuanced language using few-shot prompts
classification batch processing few-shot
07 / ORGANIZATION

Content Classifier

CMS teams, archive managers
Category
Politics
Urgency
Breaking
Tone
Investigative
Region
National
  • Multi-label classification with confidence scores
  • Custom taxonomy matching newsroom categories
  • Auto-tags content for CMS and recommendation engines
classification structured output few-shot
08 / LOCALIZATION

Translation Pipeline

BBC, Al Jazeera, DW News
Source (ES)
"El presidente anuncio nuevas medidas economicas que afectaran a millones de familias."
Translation (EN)
"The president announced new economic measures that will affect millions of families."
Confidence: 98% | Tone: Neutral | Register: Formal
  • Preserves journalistic tone and register across languages
  • Flags culturally specific terms for editor review
  • Confidence scoring with uncertain segments highlighted
translation prompt engineering quality metrics
09 / EXTRACTION

Data Extraction from PDFs

IRE, data journalism teams
PDF
Scanned budget document (image PDF)
↓
OCR
Vision model extracts tables and text
↓
CSV
Structured data output (JSON/CSV)
  • Multimodal models handle scanned/image PDFs
  • Table extraction with row/column alignment
  • Outputs structured JSON or CSV for data analysis
vision extraction structured output
10 / AUTOMATION

Automated Beat Reporter

AP Automation, LAT
1
Monitor: police logs, court filings, agendas
↓
2
Detect: newsworthy events above threshold
↓
3
Draft: structured brief for editor review
  • Monitors public data feeds for newsworthy events
  • Generates structured story briefs, not finished articles
  • Human editor reviews and decides on coverage
agents monitoring structured output
11 / RESEARCH

Research Assistant

Investigative teams, researchers
Reporter
What are the campaign finance records for candidates in District 5?
AI Assistant
Searching FEC records and state filing database...

Found 3 candidates with filings. Top donor: PAC XYZ ($125K). Notable: One candidate has no itemized individual donations.
  • Connects to public databases via tool use
  • Surfaces patterns and anomalies in the data
  • Maintains conversation history for iterative research
tool use RAG agents
12 / CREATIVE

Story Lead Generator

Local newsrooms, tip lines
Lead Score: High
City council quietly approved a $2M contract with a company owned by a council member's spouse. Found in meeting minutes from March 4.
Sources: City meeting minutes, state business registry, campaign finance records
  • Cross-references public records for potential stories
  • Detects conflicts of interest, unusual patterns
  • Ranks leads by newsworthiness and public impact
agents data mining tool use
13 / SEARCH

Public Records Search

Court reporters, data teams
Q
"Show me all property transfers over $1M in 2024"
↓
AI
Converts to SQL, queries county recorder database
↓
R
Returns 47 results with linked deed documents
  • Natural language to SQL/API query translation
  • Searches across property, court, business, and campaign databases
  • Returns results with links to original documents
text-to-SQL tool use database access
14 / ACCESSIBILITY

Image Description for Accessibility

All newsrooms (ADA compliance)
Image Input
[Photo of a crowd gathered at a city hall building]
Alt Text
"Approximately 200 people gather on the steps of Springfield City Hall during a rally for affordable housing reform on Tuesday, March 12, 2024. Several attendees hold signs reading 'Housing is a Right.'"
  • Vision model generates journalistic alt text descriptions
  • Follows AP style for image captions and descriptions
  • Includes relevant context: who, what, where, when
vision accessibility prompt engineering
15 / ANALYTICS

Audience Analytics

Digital editors, engagement teams
Readability
8.2
Engagement
7.2
SEO Score
6.4
Shareability
7.8
  • Predicts article performance using content analysis
  • Suggests headline variants optimized for engagement
  • Analyzes readability level for target audience
analytics prediction optimization
